title: Dissolution Equilibrium Visualizer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Animate sparingly soluble salts dissolving, track the ion product Q vs Ksp, and demonstrate the common-ion effect with responsive controls.

learningObjectives: Explain how dissolution reaches dynamic equilibrium where Q = Ksp.
Predict how adding a common ion or extra solid shifts Q relative to Ksp.
prerequisites: Qualitative understanding of the common-ion effect

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Animated particle model with live charts
keyQuestions: What happens to Q as dissolution proceeds?
How does a common ion change the equilibrium concentrations?
commonMisconceptions: Adding more solid always increases solubility.
Common ions raise Ksp rather than shifting Q.
assessmentOpportunities: Students log Q values before/after common-ion addition.
Learners explain why precipitation resumes when Q > Ksp.
